rationale: Existing literature describes paper-based immunosensors for hs-CRP, but these are often colorimetric or require sample preprocessing. Electrochemical methods for CRP detection are also documented but are not typically presented as paper-based platforms for direct whole blood analysis without preprocessing. The proposed combination of a paper-based electrochemical biosensor for direct, preprocessing-free whole blood detection of hs-CRP at the specified sensitivity is a novel integration.

references.title: A colorimetric paper-based immunosensor for high-sensitivity C-reactive protein (hs-CRP) detection.
references.authors: Duangtip Lawanstien, Pakakan Ngernpradab, M. Srisa-Art
references.snippet: A simple colorimetric immunosensor using a paper-based analytical device (PAD) and a sandwich immunoassay was developed for the detection of high-sensitivity C-reactive protein.

references.title: Detection of C-Reactive Protein by Electrochemiluminescence-Based Droplet-Free Digital Immunoassay with Tyramide Signal Amplification
references.authors: Hiroya Abe, S. Nakamura, Kentaro Ito, K. Ino, Zhengyi Liu, Shinichi Sato
references.snippet: C-reactive protein (CRP) is a widely used biomarker for monitoring inflammatory processes and assessing disease progression.

references.title: Eu-Chelate Polystyrene Microsphere-Based Lateral Flow Immunoassay Platform for hs-CRP Detection
references.authors: Birui Jin, Zhiguo Du, Chuyao Zhang, Zhao Yu, Xuemin Wang, Jie Hu
references.snippet: Inflammation caused by viral or bacterial infection is a major threat to human health globally.
